channel.title: Introductions (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Introductions" - New to the site? Introduce yourself and meet other site members here.

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: Have you joined the site because you're an avid reader and are planning to vote, comment, or just lurk on the site? Interested in helping develop the Next Best Thing? Post your introduction here!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what do you think is the best kind of food to eat with friends?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Explorers
item.description: Not entirely sure what you want to do now that you've joined, or are planning on contributing in an unusual way? Post your introduction here!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: what do you think is the best kind of food to eat with friends?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.description: Have you joined the site because you want to write an SCP article or tale of your own, sometime now or in the future? Post your introduction here!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: what do you think is the best kind of food to eat with friends?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: DECEMBER 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what kind of random thoughts keep you up at night?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: DECEMBER 2020 INTROS - Explorers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: what kind of random thoughts keep you up at night?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: DECEMBER 2020 INTROS - Writers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: what kind of random thoughts keep you up at night?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: NOVEMBER 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: of the classical elements (fire, water, air, earth), which represents you the most, and why?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: of the classical elements (fire, water, air, earth), which represents you the most, and why?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: NOVEMBER 2020 INTROS - Writers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: of the classical elements (fire, water, air, earth), which represents you the most, and why?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: OCTOBER 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: Have you joined the site because you're an avid reader and are planning to vote, comment, or just lurk on the site? Interested in helping develop the Next Best Thing? Post your introduction here!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what sound always puts you in a good mood?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: what sound always puts you in a good mood?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: OCTOBER 2020 INTROS - Writers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: what sound always puts you in a good mood?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: SEPTEMBER 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: would you keep a miniature version of your favorite SCP in your house? Why or why not?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: would you keep a miniature version of your favorite SCP in your house? Why or why not?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: would you keep a miniature version of your favorite SCP in your house? Why or why not?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: AUGUST 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what is your favorite way to relax?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: what is your favorite way to relax?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! item.title: AUGUST 2020 INTROS - Writers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, prospective site writers! It's good to see you here!

This month's random question is: what is your favorite way to relax?

First off, a couple standard things to keep in mind as you get started towards adding your voice to the Foundation universe:

  • Definitely read the How to Write an SCP Guide and the pages in the Guide Hub that are relevant to your ideas!
  • Make a sandbox page for your works in progress, reference notes, inspirational snippets, and more author things!
  • Get familiar with our three Writing Help Forums:
    • Questions Desk: For all your questions about the writing process!
    • Ideas and Brainstorming: For asking about concepts that you're thinking of writing up! This is an especially important step for first-time authors!
    • Drafts and Critiques: For reviews of your finished pieces, to see what edits need to be made to your work to make it ready for the mainlist!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Feel free to respond to other new members if you find that your writing interests and/or plans overlap. The SCP wiki is a collaborative writing community, after all! You might even see some of the longtime writers here drop by with comments now and then. :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: JULY 2020 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, readers and reviewers of the Foundation Universe!

This month's random question is: what is your favorite homemade food?

Let's start off with some things to take a look at to enhance your reading, now that you're a site member:

  • If you have any questions about the Foundation'verse and its various individuals and groups of interest, head over to the Foundation Universe forum to talk about the various aspects of living/working/being in constant fear of a world where anomalies are real.
  • Did you know our site has a night mode version? If you're interested in that (and a few other neat things!), check out our Usertools page.
  • Want to leave a comment or read others' thoughts on something you've read recently? Scroll down to the bottom of that page you read, click the "Discuss" link, and take a look at the comments others have posted!

For those looking to put your knowledge of the SCP-verse to work and contribute to the site by helping develop works-in-progress, head on over to the Writing Help forums!

  • So you want to be a concept reviewer?
  • So you want to be a draft reviewer?
  • And of course, remember the Criticism Policy!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Go ahead and respond to other new members if you find that your interests overlap, and definitely ask for reading suggestions if there's a particular kind of SCP article or tale you enjoy reading. If you're looking to get started as a critiquer, feel free to mention the kind of material you particularly want to see more of on the wiki! You might even see some of our authors drop by here from time to time, if you mention you liked their work! :)

Welcome to the site!

item.title: JULY 2020 INTROS - Explorers
item.description: This is an old thread now! If you'd like to introduce yourself, please use a thread for the current month!
item.content:encoded:
Note: If you're going to introduce yourself here, please scroll down and click "New Post" and NOT "reply", otherwise your comment will nest incorrectly!

Hello, new site members/artists/gamers/social media watchers/roleplayers/etc.!

This month's random question is: what is your favorite homemade food?

Here's some places to talk to get you all started off, now that you've arrived:

  • Games and Hobbies forum: Just started playing a new game or are wondering if any other SCP site members play your favorite game? Alternatively, wondering if anyone else paints minifigures or goes to conventions? Jump into the conversations here.
  • Media Discussion forum: Have a cool video or news article that just screams of the paranormal? Talk about it and other such things here!
  • Fan Work forum: Have a cool drawing or want to see the work of some of the other artists here? Have an SCP-based mod or board game in the works? This is the place to discuss!

If you're not sure what to talk about here, you can take a look at our How to Write an Intro/Welcome thread for ideas.

Have fun mingling with the other new arrivals and talking about your interests, hobbies, and plans for taking over the universe. You might also see some of our veteran users here from time to time, since you never know when someone might be looking for an artist or a voice actor or an idea-haver for the next big collaboration article or SCP project! Talk and be merry. :)

Welcome to the site!
